page: Microsoft Azure D64als_v7 is a member of the Dalsv7 server family, featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. This configuration provides 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It features no local storage, though it is premium storage capable, and does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the AMD EPYC processor demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression. Multi-core capabilities are also strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression. Passmark CPU tests further validate this compute capability, with the overall CPU score and extended instructions test both reaching the top 10%. Memory subsystem performance is highly efficient, with Passmark memory scores and database operations scoring in the top 10%, while uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidths remain in the top 25%.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the D64als_v7 is its low memory density of 2.0 GB per core and the complete absence of local storage. This profile makes it highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local disk space, optimizing resource spend on raw CPU capacity. It is well-suited for tasks such as high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and CPU-bound computational tasks that leverage its advanced vector extensions.
description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v7 is a compute-focused virtual machine from the Dalsv7 family, utilizing a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features 128.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but is premium storage capable. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng, Passmark CPU, and database operations ranking in the top 10%. This hardware profile offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for compute-intensive tasks by minimizing memory overhead. It is highly suited for multi-threaded processing, web serving, and database operations that require high CPU throughput without massive memory footprints.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v7 is a compute-focused server with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, delivering top-tier single-core performance for CPU-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v7 features 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, optimized for high-compute workloads with no local storage.
tagline: A high-compute virtual server featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and a low memory-to-core ratio for optimized processing efficiency.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
Features 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU
Delivers top-tier single-core performance and Passmark CPU scores in the top 10%
Lacks local storage but supports premium external storage options
Optimized for compute-heavy workloads, database operations, and high-throughput web serving
